page: Microsoft Azure E20as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU runs at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports key instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

The instance is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, which classifies it as a memory-optimized resource. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this server.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2702.97 and a strong PassMark memory score of 2832.71. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within average ranges. The system demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small LLM models, but performance drops significantly to a poor rating of 4.15 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large 70B models, highlighting a clear limitation for heavy AI workloads without GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20as_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine configured with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz. Utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive applications. It features no local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and lacks bundled GPUs or public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. However, LLM inference performance on large models is poor due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This instance is best suited for memory-optimized workloads such as database management, in-memory caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without local storage dependencies.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 160 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance and high memory density for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 160 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high memory density for demanding enterprise database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 20-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU
No local storage included, but supports Premium Storage configurations
Strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications
